items.title: Treasure logs 100m views with ‘If I’ music video
items.snippet: Treasure garnered 100 million views on YouTube with the music video for “If I” as of Saturday, its label YG Entertainment said the following day. The views reached the milestone in...
items.description: Treasure garnered 100 million views on YouTube with the music video for “If I” as of Saturday, its label YG Entertainment said the following day. The views reached the milestone in less than 12 days, the fastest time for the group. “If I” is the main track from the group's fourth EP “New Wav,” which came out on June 1. The four-track mini album sold more than 1 million copies in the first week, and topped the iTunes Top Albums Chart in 13 regions, as well as Oricon’s Weekly Album Ranking in Japa

items.title: BTS transforms Busan into citywide festival with drone shows and fan events
items.snippet: BUSAN — Busan turned red for BTS over the weekend, as the city’s landmarks, beaches and night sky became part of a large-scale celebration marking the group’s first concert in the...
items.description: BUSAN — Busan turned red for BTS over the weekend, as the city’s landmarks, beaches and night sky became part of a large-scale celebration marking the group’s first concert in the city in four years and its 13th debut anniversary. Through “BTS The City Arirang — Busan,” Korea's second-largest city was transformed into an extension of the group’s ongoing “Arirang” world tour. items.title: BTS marks 13th anniversary with emotional Busan homecoming
items.snippet: BUSAN — BTS wrapped up a two-day concert in Busan on Saturday that felt less like another stop on its ongoing “Arirang” world tour and more like a homecoming years in the making. T...
items.description: BUSAN — BTS wrapped up a two-day concert in Busan on Saturday that felt less like another stop on its ongoing “Arirang” world tour and more like a homecoming years in the making. The second and final night, held at Busan Asiad Main Stadium on the group’s 13th debut anniversary, June 13, drew approximately 110,000 fans. The sold-out two-day run attracted a combined audience of about 220,000. The concerts marked BTS’ first return to Busan since 2022’s “Yet To Come in Busan,” a performance remember

items.title: BTS buses, blimp set Busan concert opening abuzz
items.snippet: Busan was awash in BTS fever on Friday, on the eve of the group’s 13th debut anniversary, as fans from all over the world gathered, and themed displays — including a blimp and buse...
items.description: Busan was awash in BTS fever on Friday, on the eve of the group’s 13th debut anniversary, as fans from all over the world gathered, and themed displays — including a blimp and buses wrapped with members' pictures — marked the opening of the group's two-day concert. Ahead of BTS' two-day "Arirang" world tour stop at Busan Asiad Main Stadium, the group said it was "very happy" to reunite with its fans, known collectively as Army, in Korea. items.title: [Exclusive] Big Bang preparing August return with physical single
items.snippet: Big Bang is expected to return with a physical single featuring around two to three new songs as part of its 20th-anniversary comeback in August, industry sources said Wednesday. W...
items.description: Big Bang is expected to return with a physical single featuring around two to three new songs as part of its 20th-anniversary comeback in August, industry sources said Wednesday. While the group has already hinted at new music and a world tour this year, sources told The Korea Herald that a single release currently appears more likely than a full-length album. "Big Bang will likely release new songs in a single format with around three tracks," one source said on condition of anonymity. The sour

items.title: 'Golden' artist Ejae performs as World Cup opens in Mexico City
items.snippet: Ejae, the Korean American singer-songwriter behind "Golden" from Netflix's "KPop Demon Hunters," performed the official 2026 FIFA World Cup anthem "DNA" alongside Italian tenor And...
items.description: Ejae, the Korean American singer-songwriter behind "Golden" from Netflix's "KPop Demon Hunters," performed the official 2026 FIFA World Cup anthem "DNA" alongside Italian tenor Andrea Bocelli on Thursday at the tournament's opening ceremony at Estadio Azteca in Mexico City. The performance marked the live debut of the song, which FIFA released Wednesday. French DJ David Guetta and American rapper Megan Thee Stallion also feature on the recording, but were not in attendance. Ejae wrote the anthem

items.title: BoyNextDoor tops Oricon chart with 1st LP
items.snippet: BoyNextDoor entered Oricon’s Daily Album Ranking at No. 1 with first studio album “Home,” KOZ Entertainment has announced, citing the chart dated June 10. The LP is closing in on t...
items.description: BoyNextDoor entered Oricon’s Daily Album Ranking at No. 1 with first studio album “Home,” KOZ Entertainment has announced, citing the chart dated June 10. The LP is closing in on the 1 million sales mark, surpassing 970,000 as of Thursday. Three preceding sets from the group of six — its third to fifth EPs — were all million-sellers. items.title: Epex to host concert tour next month
items.snippet: Boy group Epex will hold concerts in Seoul, Tokyo and Taipei, Taiwan from mid-July, C9 Entertainment announced Friday. The group will perform in Seoul on July 18 and 19, before sho...
items.description: Boy group Epex will hold concerts in Seoul, Tokyo and Taipei, Taiwan from mid-July, C9 Entertainment announced Friday. The group will perform in Seoul on July 18 and 19, before shows in Tokyo and Taipei in the following months, for the tour named after the seventh EP, "Youth: Epilogue." The 5-year-old group brought out the mini album on June 9, an epilogue concluding its "Youth" trilogy. items.title: BTS to launch Busan shows for 'Arirang' world tour
items.snippet: K-pop supergroup BTS will begin the Busan leg of its "Arirang" world tour Friday, returning to the southern South Korean port city, alongside its global fan base, known as ARMY. Th...
items.description: K-pop supergroup BTS will begin the Busan leg of its "Arirang" world tour Friday, returning to the southern South Korean port city, alongside its global fan base, known as ARMY. The group will perform at Busan Asiad Main Stadium on Friday and Saturday, with the shows expected to draw a combined audience of around 110,000. Tickets for both shows have sold out. The K-pop septet kicked off the tour in April at Goyang Stadium in Goyang, just northwest of Seoul, where it had three shows before headin

items.title: BTS to release new digital single 'Come Over'
items.snippet: K-pop supergroup BTS will release the new digital single "Come Over" on Thursday, the group's agency BigHit Music said. The track was previously included on the deluxe vinyl editio...
items.description: K-pop supergroup BTS will release the new digital single "Come Over" on Thursday, the group's agency BigHit Music said. The track was previously included on the deluxe vinyl edition of the band's fifth studio album, "Arirang," released last month. The song will be made available on streaming platforms at 1 p.m. as part of the group's annual "BTS Festa" event marking its debut anniversary. items.title: BTS took a bold but necessary gamble with 'Swim,' Karlsson says
items.snippet: Swedish songwriter and producer Alex Karlsson has spent more than a decade helping shape the sound of K-pop from behind the scenes. Since beginning his collaboration with SM Entert...
items.description: Swedish songwriter and producer Alex Karlsson has spent more than a decade helping shape the sound of K-pop from behind the scenes. Since beginning his collaboration with SM Entertainment in 2013, Karlsson has infused elements of Swedish pop songwriting into numerous K-pop releases. His credits span some of the industry's biggest acts, including BTS' "We Are Bulletproof: The Eternal," SuperM's "Tiger Inside," Enhypen's "Fever," Tomorrow X Together's "Loser=Lover" and Ateez's "Bouncy (K-Hot Chill
